name: Cafe Au Lait Cheesecake
recipeIngredient: 1 3/4 cups Finely crushed chocolate wafers - about 30
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: Better Homes and Gardens magazine, Feb.'95
Crust:
In a mixing bowl, combine the crushed chocolate wafers and melted margarine or butter. Press the mixture evenly onto the bottom and 2/3 up the sides of an ungreased 8-inch springform pan. Place the pan on a baking sheet. Chill until ready.
Chocolate-coffee Mixture:
Combine the chopped semisweet chocolate, water, and instant espresso coffee powder in a small saucepan. Cook over low heat, stirring occasionally, until the chocolate begins to melt. Remove from heat and stir until smooth. Stir in the coffee liqueur or water and let cool.
Cream Cheese Mixture:
In a large mixing bowl, beat the cream cheese, sugar, flour, and vanilla extract with an electric mixer on medium speed until smooth. Add the eggs one at a time, beating on low speed just until combined. Do not overbeat. Reserve 2 cups of the cream cheese mixture.
Assembling and Baking:
Stir the cooled chocolate-coffee mixture into the remaining cream cheese mixture, stirring just until combined. Pour this into the crust. Bake in a 350°F (175°C) oven for 30 minutes or until the sides are set (the center will remain soft). Remove the reserved mixture from the refrigerator 10 minutes before baking.
Gently remove the oven rack so you can reach the inside of the pan. Carefully pour the reserved mixture into the center of the chocolate layer, spreading it evenly over the entire surface. Bake for an additional 20-25 minutes or until the center appears nearly set when gently shaken. Let cool on a wire rack for 10 minutes.
Loosen the sides of the cheesecake from the pan and let cool completely. Chill the cheesecake for 4-24 hours before serving. Store any remaining cheesecake, covered in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
To Freeze:
Place the cooled cheesecake, uncovered, in the freezer for about an hour until firm. Remove the bottom of the pan. Transfer the cheesecake to a large freezer bag or container. Seal, label, and freeze up to 3 months. To serve, transfer the cheesecake to a platter and loosely cover. Thaw for 24 hours in the refrigerator.
